mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress consumer spending expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.5pp within a single month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which compounds corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion phase signals supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases produce positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates real rate compression that boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds CPIAUCSL annualized change by > 1.0pp
failure_condition: The spread between nominal rates and inflation fails to predict 20-day returns with a positive sign across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in total nonfarm payrolls reduce aggregate income growth and corporate revenue forecasts, systematically generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines correlate with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in > 50% of monthly observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent confirms robust economic momentum that immediately lifts equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signal that higher borrowing costs are successfully suppressing output, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles yield positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances.
